clean_text: Old Cuban Cocktail Recipe | How to make a Old Cuban | BACARDÍ Global OUR RUMS OUR RUMS White Rum BACARDÍ CARTA BLANCA Flavors Flavors Range Spiced BACARDÍ Spiced Dark Rum BACARDÍ Carta Oro BACARDÍ Carta Negra BACARDÍ Añejo Cuatro BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Diez BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Limitada ready to enjoy BACARDÍ x coca-cola Ready to Drink cocktails Ready to Serve cocktails BACARDÍ Coquito View all our rums Cocktails Experiences Experiences Casa BACARDÍ Puerto Rico Visit the World’s largest premium rum distillery. SUSTAINABILITY Our World FAQS BASE RUM BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho rum COCKTAILS Old Cuban Inspired by BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho dark rum, legendary NYC mixologist Audrey Saunders created a new cocktail to showcase it. A sparkling combination of rum and sparkling wine, the Old Cuban is on its way to becoming a modern classic. VIDEO HOW TO MAKE A OLD CUBAN A sparkling combination of BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho rum and prosecco. How to make a Old Cuban 45 ml BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho rum 60 ml MARTINI Prosecco sparkling wine 6 mint leaves 25 ml simple syrup 20 ml lime juice 2 dashes Angostura® bitters Servings Copy Old Cuban ingredients Copied! Old Cuban Recipe One step at a time 1 Combine Combine all the ingredients (except prosecco) in a cocktail shaker with ice. 2 Shake Shake vigorously. 3 Strain Double strain, and top with prosecco. 4 Garnish Garnish with a mint leaf float. MERCHANDISE GET KITTED OUT Glasses. Squeezers. Cocktail kits. Discover everything you need to create standout BACARDÍ cocktails. SHOP NOW OUR RECOMMENDED RUM COCKTAILS Like This? Try These If this is your kind of cocktail, here’s our selection of other BACARDÍ cocktails that might suit your taste VIEW ALL RUM COCKTAILS Ocho Old Fashioned Rum Negroni Hotel Nacional Ocho on the Rocks Diez Neat DRAG TO DISCOVER MORE VIEW ALL RUM COCKTAILS FAQ WANT TO KNOW MORE? How do you make rum? Rum is made using only three ingredients: molasses from sugarcane, yeast and water. Ingredients are fermented, then distilled, aged, filtered and blended. What is the difference between dark, gold and white rum? Rum is a wonderfully complex and diverse spirit. Color can give us clues about time spent aging or the flavours that might be found within. Dark can be bold, sweet (or dry) and even smoky, gold is rich and soothing, and white is smooth, subtle and often refreshing. But color can be misleading. The best way to truly understand a rum is to taste it for yourself. SOCIAL MOVE WITH US A soft sway, or a lively dance. We have something for everyone.
